source_pr.title: executor: refactor some methods of `Row` and `Chunk`
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->
Refactor some code of `Row`, which is prepared for introducing `Sel` to `Chunk`.

### What is changed and how it works?
1. hide some unsafe methods of `Chunk` which only used in tests;
2. refactor `Row` to use vectorized `Column` API;
3. add some methods for `Column`;

All changes are refactoring or new methods, so it's easy to review.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test

symptoms: // when the Insert() function is called parallelly, the data race on a byte
failure_signature: // when the Insert() function is called parallelly, the data race on a byte
root_cause_explanation: The flakiness risk here is a Go DATA RACE when manipulating a shared `Chunk` from multiple goroutines: `PreAlloc`/`Insert` update row null-bitmaps at the byte level, so parallel inserts can race even if goroutines write different rows. This PR mitigates the risk by hiding these unsafe APIs (make them unexported and explicitly documented as test-only), reducing the chance they are used in concurrent code paths without proper synchronization.
fix_pattern: Avoid mutating a `Chunk` concurrently from multiple goroutines; hide/limit unsafe helper APIs and require explicit synchronization or redesigned data flow when parallel writes are needed.

evidence: evidence: // when the Insert() function is called parallelly, the data race on a byte
files: util/chunk/chunk.go, util/chunk/chunk_test.go, util/chunk/list.go, util/chunk/list_test.go, util/chunk/column.go, util/chunk/column_test.go, util/chunk/row.go
